_comment: OC Pledge example — open-source delivery. A maintainer commits to publishing a v1.0 release tarball at a canonical URL with a specific SHA-256 by a deadline. Resolution is deterministic: any verifier curls the URL, hashes the body, compares. No signature needed at the outcome envelope layer — the deterministic mechanism produces the same byte-identical envelope from every verifier with the same public state. If the URL 404s or hashes wrong on or before resolves_at, the deterministic outcome is `broken` and the pledge attaches to bc1qdave's address as broken forever. The bond (750K sats × 60 days) is a real signal of commitment; no protocol actor can seize it. To run this example: (1) BIP-322-sign the canonical message, (2) publish to Nostr kind-30078 d-tag oc-pledge:<id>, (3) at the deadline, any verifier classifies the pledge automatically.
_resolves_to: deterministic kept iff URL body sha256 matches expected; deterministic broken otherwise
proposition: I will publish the v1.0 release tarball at the canonical URL with sha256:abc123… by 2026-07-15.

sig.value: <base64 BIP-322 signature by bc1qdave over hex pledge_id>
